questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Given the $E_0$ values for the half reactions:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{Sn}^{4+}+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Sn}^{2+}, 0.15 \mathrm{~V} \\
& 2 \mathrm{Hg}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Hg}_2^{2+}, 0.92 \mathrm{~V} \\
& \mathrm{PbO}_2+4 \mathrm{H}^{+}+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Pb}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}, 1.45 \mathrm{~V}
\end{aligned}$
Which of the following statements is true?
options: $\mathrm{Sn}^{2+}$ is a stronger oxidizing agent than $\mathrm{Pb}^{4+}$.
$\mathrm{Sn}^{2+}$ is a stronger reducing agent than $\mathrm{Hg}_2^{2+}$.
$\mathrm{Hg}^{2+}$ is a stronger oxidizing agent than $\mathrm{Pb}^{4+}$.
$\mathrm{Pb}^{2+}$ is a stronger reducing agent than $\mathrm{Sn}^{2+}$.

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Which of the following statements is not correct regarding the galvanic cells?
options: Ions carry current inside the cell.
Electrons flow in the external circuit from cathode to anode.
When the cell potential is positive, the cell reaction is spontaneous.

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: NaOH solution is added dropwise to HCl solution and the conductance of the mixture is measured after addition of each drop. The variation of conductance with volume of NaOH added is as shown in adjacent graph. The statement that is not true for the above is
options: decrease in conductance from $\mathrm{X} \rightarrow \mathrm{Y}$ is due to decrease in $\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]$.
point Y represents the eqivalence point of titration.
segment YZ represents the conductance due to ions from NaCl and NaOH in solution.

questions.q:

$\begin{array}{ll}\mathrm{Sn}^{4+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Sn}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq}) & \mathrm{E}^0=0.15 \mathrm{~V} \\ \mathrm{Br}_2(\mathrm{l})+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Br}^{-}(\mathrm{aq}) & \mathrm{E}^0=1.07 \mathrm{~V}\end{array}$

questions.q: Concentration of $\mathrm{K}^{+}$ions inside a biological cell was found to be 25 times higher than that outside. Among the following, the cell that can produce an EMF more than that of the Galvanic cell is ( $\mathrm{E}^0$ of $\mathrm{Zn}^{2+} / \mathrm{Zn}$ and $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+} / \mathrm{Cu}$ are -0.763 V and 0.337 V respectively)

The molar conductivity of NaCl at this concentration is $10 \Omega^{-1} \mathrm{~m}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$. The volume of water in the water tank is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: When the pH of the system is increased by 2.0 units, maximum decrease in half cell potential for the reaction will be observed in

questions.subject: NSEC
NSEC
questions.q: Electrolysis of aqueous $\mathrm{CuSO}_4(0.1 \mathrm{M})$ was carried out in two cells I and II. In I, the electrodes are of Cu and in II they were of Pt . As the electrolysis proceeds pH of the electrolyte solution will:
options: decrease in II and remain the same in I
remain the same in both I and II
increase in both I and II
increase in I and decrease in II

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
A half-cell reaction represented by (i) as given below
$\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{OH})_2(\mathrm{~s})+2 e^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{OH}^{-}(\mathrm{aq}) \quad E^{\ominus}=-0.9 \mathrm{~V} \mathrm{(i)}$
takes place in two different electrochemical cells, I and II, in which the other half cell reactions are (ii) and (iii) respectively:
$\begin{array}{ll}
A l^{3+}(a q)+3 e^{-} \rightarrow A l(s) & E^{\ominus}=-1.7 \mathrm{~V} \mathrm{(ii)} \\
A g B r(s)+e^{-} \rightarrow A g(s)+B r^{-}(a q) & E^{\ominus}=-0.07 \mathrm{~V} \mathrm{(iii)}
\end{array}$
The correct option that represents the redox reactions in cells I and II is:
options: Fe is oxidised in cell I; Fe is oxidised in cell II
Fe is oxidised in cell I; Fe is reduced in cell II
Fe is reduced in cell $\mathrm{I} ; \mathrm{Fe}$ is reduced in cell II
Fe is reduced in cell I; Fe is oxidised in cell II

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Latimer diagrams are the compact representations of electrochemical equilibria in substances of multiple oxidation states. The value of the potential, $x$, in the Latimer diagram of gold (at $\mathrm{pH}=1.0$ ) is:
